name: phylogenetics
tagline: Local bioinformatics pipeline (MAFFT, IQ-TREE 2, FastTree, ETE3) for building and analyzing phylogenetic trees via subprocess calls.
verdict: Clean skill: shells out only to locally installed CLI tools (mafft, iqtree2, FastTree, trimal) on user files, reads no credentials, makes no network calls. Minor gaps: TrimAl missing from install block, IQ-TREE's --redo overwrites prior results silently, no bootstrap seed, no 'when NOT to use' guidance. No safety risk to install.

highlights.summary: run_iqtree() always passes --redo (line 145, comment 'Overwrite existing results'), and trim_alignment_trimal()'s failure path silently shutil.copy()'s over output_fasta (lines 111-113) — both overwri
No explicit 'do not use this for X' guidance anywhere in the file — e.g. no note that this isn't for pairwise alignment only, small-scale BLAST lookups, or non-phylogenetic sequence tasks.

No random seed parameter is exposed or set for IQ-TREE's ultrafast bootstrap or MAFFT's iterative refinement, so identical inputs can yield slightly different bootstrap/tree output across runs — not c
